name: Temporary Housing Foundation
description_short: HUD-certified nonprofit housing counselor in Centre County, PA offering budget, credit, and homebuyer counseling plus affordable housing development since 1988.
description_long:
The HOME Foundation (originally Temporary Housing Foundation) was incorporated in 1988 as the fundraising arm for what is now Housing Transitions, a nonprofit addressing homelessness in Centre County, Pennsylvania. It began by providing emergency shelter and meals, then evolved into a Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) in the late 1990s with access to federal funding for affordable housing initiatives.

The organization provides HUD-certified housing counseling services across a full spectrum of needs. Their 2 HUD Certified Housing Counselors specialize in rental counseling and the Homeowner Emergency Mortgage Assistance Program (HEMAP), but also offer budget and credit counseling, first-time homebuyer education, and pre- and post-purchase counseling. They serve clients through the State College Borough Home Partnership Program and earned HUD Certified Housing Counseling Agency status in April 2020.

Beyond counseling, HOME Foundation distinguishes itself through direct affordable housing development. It has acquired and sold over 30 homes to qualified buyers at affordable prices and currently owns rental properties including two duplexes (each with two 3-bedroom units) and two larger apartment buildings with a combined 17 units in State College Borough. This dual approach—combining counseling with hands-on housing solutions—sets them apart from typical credit counseling nonprofits.

As a free-help resource, HOME Foundation is strongest for individuals needing housing-focused financial guidance and first-time homebuyers in Centre County. However, their geographic service area appears limited to State College Borough and Centre County, and they do not appear to offer broader credit repair, debt consolidation, or emergency lending services.
pros: HUD Certified Housing Counseling Agency status (as of April 2020) with 2 certified counselors on staff
Specialization in Homeowner Emergency Mortgage Assistance Program (HEMAP) for homeowners in distress
Serves as Community Housing Development Organization with direct experience acquiring, rehabilitating, and selling 30+ affordable homes
Comprehensive counseling services including budget, credit, rental, pre-purchase, and post-purchase counseling
Long operational history since 1988 demonstrating sustained community commitment
Owns and manages affordable rental properties (4 buildings, 17+ units) providing real-world housing solutions
Participation in statewide initiatives including ECHO cottage housing and Energy+ efficiency programs
cons: Service area appears limited to Centre County, Pennsylvania (State College Borough and surrounding area)
No indication of online or remote counseling services despite HUD certification that could enable broader reach
Website does not clearly specify hours, appointment availability, or response times for counseling requests
Limited information about wait times or accessibility for urgent housing situations beyond HEMAP program
Does not appear to offer credit repair, debt consolidation, or emergency financial assistance—only counseling
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Centre County seeking pre-purchase education and down payment assistance guidance
Homeowners facing mortgage payment difficulties eligible for HEMAP assistance in Pennsylvania
Renters in State College Borough needing rental counseling and housing stability services
Individuals seeking budget and credit counseling from a HUD-certified nonprofit with affordable housing expertise
diagnosis: HOME Foundation is best for Centre County residents seeking HUD-certified housing counseling, particularly first-time homebuyers and homeowners facing mortgage distress. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Centre County, PA, and the absence of broader consumer credit services like credit repair or debt relief—this is a housing-focused counselor, not a general credit counseling agency.
services: Homeowner Emergency Mortgage Assistance Program (HEMAP) counseling
Community Housing Development Organization (CHDO) services including home purchase and rehabilitation
Affordable home sales to qualified buyers
Participation in Energy+ initiative for energy-efficient housing retrofits
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 226 E Nittany Ave, State College, PA 16801
